headline: Sprouts Farmers Market (SFM) Takes Over A Never Opened Amazon Fresh Store
summary: Sprouts Farmers Market is taking over a never-opened Amazon Fresh grocery store in Pennsylvania. The site is a fully renovated, fitted grocery location that Amazon exited before opening to the public. The transfer bypasses typical restrictive covenants that often limit successor grocery operators. This marks the first time Sprouts has directly benefited from Amazon stepping back from physical grocery. Sprouts Farmers Market, traded as NasdaqGS:SFM, is using this Amazon Fresh exit to...

headline: Why the Current Selloff in Big Tech Stocks Is a Springboard for a New Rally
summary: The recent Big Tech correction is a healthy reset, not a market crash. Big Tech capex remains the S&P 500's core engine. With falling gas prices driving down headline CPI and an impending dovish Fed pivot, current market levels offer an ideal entry point for a new historic rally.

headline: Meta’s Bold $6.5 Billion Power Move to Turbocharge Its Cloud and AI Takeover
summary: The AI arms race has entered a new phase. For the past three years, the biggest technology companies have competed by buying as many Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) GPUs as they could get their hands on. Now they’re racing to build something even more valuable: their own AI chips. That shift is about more than lowering costs. ... headline: Which Retailer Can Deliver My Order the Fastest?
summary: Investing.com -- Delivery speed is emerging as a key competitive advantage for retailers as AI-powered shopping assistants become more common, with Morgan Stanley’s latest analysis finding Walmart offers the strongest overall fulfillment model. At the same time, Amazon Now leads on raw delivery speed.

headline: Anthropic quietly joins the race to build its own chips
summary: Anthropic has opened early talks with Samsung Electronics to manufacture a custom AI chip, according to a report from Bloomberg. The Claude developer has never built its own silicon before. It has relied entirely on chips rented from Amazon, Google, and Nvidia, and that dependence is now colliding ...
